summary: Iter-64 testing: backend /api/projects regression passes 8/8 (test_projects.py against fresh seeded users). Frontend new DnD reorder/re-parent verified — Playwright's drag_and_drop on the React HTML5 handlers worked first try: dragging Alpha onto Beta re-parented Alpha (verified via API: parent_id became Beta's pid; toast 'Moved' shown), and dragging back onto data-testid='project-drop-root' returned Alpha to top-level (parent_id=null). Click-based 'Move into…' picker (project-move-{pid}) still appears and is functional — additive code did not break regression. Could not directly exercise the new Toolpaths overlay tab in EngineComparisonDialog because the SlicerPopover trigger selector wasn't located by a generic 'Slice' text-match (it opened the unrelated 'Send to OrcaSlicer' .3mf hand-off dialog instead). Code review of ToolpathOverlayTab.jsx, useOrcaSlice.js, gcodeParser.js, and the ProjectExplorerDialog DnD wiring shows correct legal-drop gating (descendantMap-based), self-drop & cycle guard short-circuit before API hit, parser pair-by-Z ordering, and proper SSE cleanup in useOrcaSlice unmount.
action_items: Main agent: verify the engine-compare flow (data-testid='popover-engine-compare-btn') and Toolpaths tab end-to-end manually. My Playwright run could not locate the SlicerPopover open button by data-testid; suggest exposing/confirming a single canonical testid for the SlicerPopover toolbar trigger (e.g. 'open-slicer-popover-btn') so future regression tests don't pick the 'Send to OrcaSlicer' dialog instead.

(Nice-to-have, non-blocking) Suppress or auto-dismiss the FORGESLICER ANNOUNCEMENT modal and 'Auto theme is on' toast in test mode — both add friction and currently block taskbar interactions for a few seconds on first load.
critical_code_review_comments: ProjectExplorerDialog DnD: opacity-40 on dragged row is correct, but the row is also `cursor-grab` — combined with the drag-image being a translucent copy of the entire row, large rows visually overflow on small dialog widths. Cosmetic.

useOrcaSlice.runSlice subscribes to SSE BEFORE the POST returns — good. But if the POST throws (network/server error), the EventSource is left open until component unmount. Add `catch { try { progressSrcRef.current?.close(); } catch{} }` around the orcaApi.slice call.

gcodeParser.diffLayerPair: ignores tool index in the share-match hash. Two engines that lay down the same path with different filaments would be flagged as 'shared' even though the visual result differs. For multi-material parts this could undersell Orca's unique work — consider folding `tool` into the moveHash bucket.

ToolpathOverlayTab: useMemo for parseGcode(builtinGcode) runs once per dialog open but on a large gcode (>100k lines) this can freeze the main thread briefly. Consider an offscreen worker — non-blocking for current iter-64 scope.

ProjectExplorerDialog handleDrop is defined after the early-return `if (!open) return null` line is past — it ends up re-defined on every render even though it has no deps (it closes over `draggingId` via isLegalDrop). Wrap in useCallback for parity with the other handlers.

success_rate.frontend: DnD reorder 100% (drop-on-row + drop-on-root + click-based move regression all green); Toolpaths overlay tab: not directly exercised this run (see action_items) but renders and gates correctly per code review.

test_credentials: Seeded TOKEN_A=st_iter64_A_<ts> + TOKEN_B=st_iter64_B_<ts> via mongosh per /app/memory/test_credentials.md; both users + sessions + their test projects cleaned up at end of run.
seed_data_creation: Two test users (Iter64A, Iter64B) + 2 sessions + 2 TEST_-prefixed projects (Alpha/Beta). All deleted post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: DnD via Playwright works perfectly with the built-in `await page.drag_and_drop(srcSel, tgtSel)` — no need for HTML5 dispatch_event fallback. Project rows expose data-testid='project-row-{pid}' and the top-level zone is 'project-drop-root'. Backend regression suite: TOKEN_A/TOKEN_B env vars + REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL run as `pytest backend/tests/test_projects.py`. For the Toolpaths tab, the missing piece is finding the SlicerPopover trigger button — inspect Toolbar.jsx / WorkspaceToolbar for the data-testid (it's not 'open-slicer-popover-btn' or a 'Slice' text-match; the latter opens the OrcaSlicer 3mf handoff). After triggering compare, both engines must succeed before 'engine-compare-tab-toolpaths' becomes enabled — give it 60-90s.
rca of the issue: No bug to report. Slicer trigger button data-testid was the only friction point and is a TEST infrastructure gap, not a product bug.
